text.title: OpenAI Fine-tuning Data Preparation
text.summary: JSONL format spec for OpenAI fine-tuning, create_training_example helper, dataset serialization, and the quality checklist for training data.

rule.statement: Every training example must be a JSON object with a "messages" key containing at least one "user" and one "assistant" turn.
rule.rationale: OpenAI rejects files missing the assistant turn; the validation API error is non-descriptive and wastes upload time.

rule.statement: Keep the system prompt identical across ALL training examples and match it exactly to the system prompt used in production inference.
rule.rationale: Divergence between training and inference system prompts is the most common cause of unexpected fine-tuned model behavior.

example.note: One JSON object per line. System prompt is a parameter — caller ensures consistency.

antipattern.description: Using different system prompts across training examples (e.g., varying the company name or tone).
antipattern.reason: The model learns to associate different behaviors with different prompts, producing unpredictable results at inference with a single system prompt.

rule.statement: Use a minimum of 50-100 examples; for meaningful improvement on complex tasks, target 500+.
rule.rationale: Below 50 examples the fine-tuned model may not meaningfully differ from the base model on the evaluation set.

rule.statement: Split dataset 80% training / 20% held-out test; never evaluate on training data.
rule.rationale: Training data evaluation produces artificially high metrics; the held-out test set is the only unbiased quality signal.

rule.statement: Have a human review a random 10% sample before submitting — quality over quantity.
rule.rationale: 200 excellent examples beat 2000 mediocre ones; automated generation without human review propagates systematic errors into training.

section.title: Decision Matrix
section.p: Use fine-tuning when the task requires consistent format/style. Use RAG when the task requires current factual knowledge. Use few-shot when you have fewer than 50 examples or need rapid iteration.
